description_long: This triumphal arch is a linear structure featuring five tall, evenly spaced arches supported by robust rectangular columns. The arches are topped with a detailed entablature that includes a row of decorative crenellations or small battlements along the upper edge. The stonework appears weathered and textured, suggesting a historical or classical influence, reminiscent of Roman aqueducts or monumental gateways. The base is a flat, dark platform that contrasts with the lighter stone of the arches, providing stability and visual separation from the ground. There are no visible sculptural reliefs or inscription panels, and the design is relatively austere, focusing on the repetition of arches and the rhythm of the supporting columns. The overall impression is one of strength, symmetry, and classical architectural order.
description: A row of five tall stone arches with rectangular columns and a crenellated entablature, set on a dark base. The design is symmetrical and classical, with a focus on structural rhythm and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing all five arches and the full length of the entablature with crenellations.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow depth of the structure and the thickness of the columns.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, displaying the five arches and entablature.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the structure's narrow profile and column thickness.
